content:
Character: Gemma Chen
Perception: Gemma is quiet and precise — small frame, wire-rimmed glasses, always carrying a bag that looks too heavy for her. She moved into the third floor in 2016, always pays rent early, and her unit is the only one that never needs a maintenance call. We've spoken perhaps a dozen times, always polite, always brief. But since late 2019, something has shifted — she looks thinner, her eyes have a hunted quality, and she's stopped coming to the rooftop entirely. I asked if everything was all right once, in the hallway, and she smiled in a way that made me more worried, not less. She sees me as her landlord. I see her as someone carrying something heavy alone. I don't know what it is, but I recognize the posture.

But since late 2019, something has shifted — she looks thinner, her eyes have a hunted quality, and she's stopped coming to the rooftop entirely. I asked if everything was all right once, in the hallway, and she smiled in a way that made me more worried, not less. She sees me as her landlord.
